fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to missouri, pharmacy technician compact missouri, missouri pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Missouri Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Missouri Board of Pharmacy
P.O. Box 625
Jefferson City, MO 65102

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Missouri.

steps.title: 1. Create Account & Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the Missouri MyLicense Online Portal (https://dpr.mo.gov/MyLicense/Login.aspx?APPTYPE=PT). Click 'Register' and 'Sign Up Now' to create your user account. Once logged in, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' to start the online application.

steps.title: 2. Complete Online Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required fields of the Pharmacy Technician application completely within the MyLicense portal. Submit the required $35 application fee as part of the online submission process.

steps.title: 3. Register for Fingerprinting
steps.description: After submitting your online application, you must register for a criminal background check (CBC) with the state's approved vendor, IDENTOGO/MACHS. Detailed instructions for registration will be provided within the application or on the Board's website (pr.mo.gov/pharmacist-tech-initial.asp).

steps.title: 4. Complete Fingerprinting
steps.description: Visit an IDENTOGO location to have your fingerprints taken. Ensure the results are directed to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy. This process must be completed after your online application submission.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review
steps.description: The Missouri Board of Pharmacy will review your submitted online application and the results of your criminal background check. This process can take several weeks.

steps.title: 6. License Issuance
steps.description: Upon successful review and approval, your Pharmacy Technician license will be issued. You can typically check the status of your application through your account on the Missouri MyLicense Online Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Missouri offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Missouri Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Missouri by submitting a new application to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Missouri-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Missouri by submitting a new application to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Missouri-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Missouri
hero.dek: Apply for a Pharmacy Technician license in Missouri through the Missouri MyLicense Online Portal (dpr.mo.gov/MyLicense/Login.aspx?APPTYPE=PT). Create an account, select the Pharmacy Technician application, and follow the steps, including completing a criminal background check.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Missouri MyLicense Online Portal, click 'Register' at the top right, then 'Sign Up Now' to create a new user account. Once logged in, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' to begin your application.
